message:
feat(scheduler): wire configurable steal_batch_size and enable_parking from RuntimeConfig

Exposes two ThreeLaneScheduler knobs through RuntimeConfig so they can
be tuned without code changes:

- steal_batch_size: controls how many ready tasks a worker steals in one
  batch from a victim's local queue (default 4, clamped to >=1). Wired
  from RuntimeConfig into builder.rs and propagated to each
  ThreeLaneWorker, replacing the hardcoded 4 in steal_ready_batch_into.
  Workers' steal_buffer capacity is adjusted to match.

- enable_parking: gates whether workers are allowed to park when idle
  (default true). When false, workers break out of the idle loop instead
  of parking, useful for latency-sensitive workloads. Wired through
  builder.rs and propagated to each ThreeLaneWorker.

Also includes:
- DEFAULT_STEAL_BATCH_SIZE and DEFAULT_ENABLE_PARKING constants
- set_steal_batch_size() and set_enable_parking() methods on
  ThreeLaneScheduler that propagate to all workers
- Updated steal batch test to use set_steal_batch_size(2) and adjusted
  assertions for configurable batch size
- Minor comment style fixes (trailing periods) in parking code paths

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
